The designation given to a school by the state for targeted support and improvement as part of its statewide system of annual meaningful differentiation.
References the exam and score of a person on a teaching credential exam.
An indication of the type of credential exam associated with a given exam score.
The type of examination used to assess teacher candidate's knowledge and skills.
The name of the company that provides the examination used in the teacher education program.

An indication of whether a person completed a state-approved teacher preparation program. The fact that a person has or has not been recommended to the state for initial certification or licensure may not be used as a criterion for determining who is a program completer.
Indicates that the student is excluded from calculation of value-added or growth attribution calculations used for teacher evaluation.
Staff member who has a Teacher of Record responsibility for a Class Section based upon the state's definition of Teacher of Record.
The role that the Staff Member has been assigned for a Class Section. (A teacher may have the lead responsibility for one section and serve a supporting role for another section of the same course.)
An indication of the technology literacy of 8th graders.
An indication that the person has achieved acceptable performance on a standards-based profile of technology user skills as defined by the state.
The child's parent or guardian is a teen parent between ages 10 to 19 years.
An indication of whether a telephone number is listed under a directory assistance service.
The type of communication number listed for a person.
An indicator of whether an institution has personnel positions that lead to consideration for tenure.
An indication of whether a Title III program or activity was terminated due to failure to meet goals.
An indication that the student is participating in and served by programs under Title I, Part A of ESEA as amended.
The type of instructional services provided to students in ESEA Title I programs.
The type of Title I program offered in the school or district.

An indication that a school is designated under state and federal regulations as being eligible for participation in programs authorized by Title I of ESEA as amended and whether it has a Title I program.
An indication of whether an eligible student applied/requested to receive supplemental educational services under Title I, Part A, Section 1116 of ESEA as amended during the school year.
An indication of whether a student is eligible to receive supplemental educational services during the school year in accordance with Title I, Part A, Section 1116 of ESEA as amended.
An indication of whether an eligible student received supplemental educational services during the school year in accordance with Title I, Part A, Section 1116 of ESEA as amended.
An indication that the student participates in and is served by a schoolwide program (SWP) under Title I of ESEA, Part A, Sections 1114.
The type of support services provided to students in Title I programs.
An indication that the student participates in and is served by a targeted assistance (TAS) program under Title I of ESEA, Part A, Sections 1115.
An indication of the progress made by a student toward English proficiency.
An indication that an English Learner student is served by an English language instruction educational program supported with Title III of ESEA funds.

An indication that an immigrant student participated in programs for immigrant children and youth funded under ESEA Title III Section 3114(d)(1) using funds reserved for immigrant education programs/activities.

An indication that the child is an immigrant according to the Title III of ESEA definition, meaning children who are aged 3 through 21; were not born in any state; and have not been attending one or more schools in any one or more States for more than 3 full academic years.
An indication of whether a teacher teaches in language instruction educational programs designed for English learners supported with Title III of ESEA, as amended by ESSA, funds.
The type of Title III professional development utilized.

A person who receives Title IV aid. Title IV aid includes grant aid, work study aid, and loan aid such as: Federal Pell Grant, Federal Supplemental Educational Opportunity Grant (FSEOG), Teacher Education Assistance for College and Higher Education (TEACH) Grant, Federal Work-Study, Federal Perkins Loan, Subsidized Direct or FFEL Stafford Loan, and Unsubsidized Direct or FFEL Stafford Loan. Title IV aid specifications are defined by the instructions for the IPEDS Student Financial Aid survey.
An indicator of whether the student has transferred to another institution.
A person who has successfully completed a transfer-preparatory program as defined by the state or by the institution if no official state definition exists.
The presentation type for the translated language.
The type of eligibility for transporting a person at public expense.
The qualification status for the transportation of a person that entitles an organization to receive state transportation aid.
The transportation status of the person.
The Native American tribal entity recognized and eligible to receive services from the United States Bureau of Indian Affairs to which a person is affiliated.
The trimester of pregnancy in which a child's mother began receiving prenatal health care.
An indication that a student is identified as a truant as defined by state rules.
A person's residency status for tuition purposes.
The component for which tuition is being charged. It might be a time period (term, quarter, year, etc.) or it might be an entity of education (course, credit hour, etc.).
The type of use of the Rural Low-Income Schools Program (RLIS) (Title VI, Part B, Subpart 2) Grant Funds.
An indication of whether the person is a member of a union.
An indicator of whether or not the person is a US citizen.
An indicator of a non-US citizen's Visa type.
Status of an examination used to measure a person's ability to see.
The disease or condition that caused the visual impairment.
A self-identified barrier as defined by the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA) that is presumed to affect placement of a participant in unsubsidized employment.

An indication of whether or not the individual has received services as identified in sec. 134(c)(2) of the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA) consisting of (a) basic career services, (b) individualized career services, or (c) follow-up services.

An indication of whether or not the individual has received services as defined by the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA), one or more courses or classes, or a structured regimen that provides the services in 20 CFR part 680.200 and leads to: (a) An industry-recognized certificate or certification, a certificate of completion of a registered apprenticeship, a license recognized by the State involved or the Federal Government, an associate or baccalaureate degree, or community college certificate of completion; (b) Consistent with � 680.350, a secondary school diploma or its equivalent; (c) Employment; or (d) Measurable skill gains toward a credential described in paragraph (a) or (b) of this section or employment.
Method used for the collection of wage data for an employment record.
An indication of whether the wage information has been verified.
A person who meets the admission requirements but will only be offered a place in the class if space becomes available.
Identifies the type of weapon used during an incident.
Indicates a student's need for a Wi-Fi enabled device to access remote instruction.
Indicates whether a student received a Wi-Fi enabled device to access remote instruction.

References person-level employment and earnings information from quarterly employment and earnings-related data from sources such as State UI Wage Records, the Wage Record Interchange System, or the Federal Employment Data Exchange System (FEDES).
References information about a specific cohort of workforce program participants.
References information about instruction and/or services provided to a person through a workforce and/or an employment development program.
The type of workforce and employment development program that an individual is participating in.
A description of the seriousness a threat or hazardous material poses.
The month, day, and year that the site or building is tested for a specific hazardous material.
The screening equipment used for the hearing screening or the method used for the vision screening
Recommendations for follow-up after a health screening.
The year, month and day of a hearing screening.
The High School Rank divided by the Size of High School Graduating Class expressed as a percentage.
The year, month and day on which a person was hired for a position, or consecutive positions within the same organization and job classification.
The year, month, and day on which the Home Language Survey is known to have been administered to the parent or guardian of this student.
A description of the type of academic distinctions earned by or awarded to the person.
Hourly wage associated with the employment position being reported.
The number of hours worked per week in employment.
The date that notification is provided to the State Education Agency (SEA) and local education agency (LEA) of residence for a child potentially eligible for Part B (619) preschool services.
The date that parents of a child potentially eligible for Part B preschool services opt out of the impending notification to the local education agency.

The rationale for the placement decision and if applicable, an explanation of the extent, if any, to which the child will not participate with nondisabled children in the regular class and in the activities described in paragraph (a)(4) of CFR. �300.320.
Portion the authorizer does not want executed.
Authorizer's explanation for rejected portions.
How the child's disability affects the child's academic achievement.
How the child's disability affects the child's functional performance.
How the child's disability affects the child's involvement and progress in the general education curriculum.
Parent's explanation of their interest in or concerns about the student's participation in special education.
For preschool children, as appropriate, how the disability affects the child's participation in appropriate activities.
Student's explanation of his or her interest in or concerns about participation in special education.
Explanation of perceived strengths and abilities of the student.

Beginning not later than one year before the child reaches the age of majority under State law, the IEP must include a statement that the child has been informed of the child's rights under Part B of the Individuals with Disabilities Education Act, if any, that will transfer to the child on reaching the age of majority under �300.520.

The academic year, generally extending from September to June, in which IPEDS data is collected.  Most Institutional Characteristics, Salaries, Fall Staff, Fall Enrollment, and Employees by Assigned Position data are collected for the current year; Completions, 12-Month Enrollment, Student Financial Aid, and Finance data collections cover the prior year.
The year, month and day of an immunization.

The value of any quantifiable monetary loss directly resulting from the incident. Examples include the value of repairs necessitated by vandalism of a school facility, the value of personnel resources used for repairs or consumed by the incident, the value of stolen items, and the value of time consumed by an incident (e.g., instructional time involved in evacuating a school during a false fire alarm).  Cost may be reported by specific monetary amount or range.
The year, month and day on which the incident occurred.
The description for an incident.

A locally assigned unique identifier (within the school or school district) to identify each specific incident or occurrence. The same identifier should be used to document the entire incident even if it included multiple offenses and multiple offenders.
Identifies the perpetrator of the incident by use of a pre-existing unique identifier assigned to a student or staff member by a school or district.
A description of the rule, regulation, or standard that was violated when an incident occurred (e.g., the identification of a relevant law, conduct standard, or acceptable use policy).
An indication of the time of day the incident took place.
Identifies the victim of the incident by use of a pre-existing unique identifier assigned to a student or staff member by a school or district.

Identifies the witness of the incident by use of a pre-existing unique identifier assigned to a student or staff member by a school or district. Using this data element permits linkage to descriptive information about the individual in the student or staff database.
The amount of indebtedness allowed by law to be carried by the school district.
A numeric value that is the rate for a given indirect cost rate type (e.g., limited) established with the cognizant agency for indirect costs or awarding agency.
The name of the Federal Agency that approved the Indirect Cost Rate Agreement.
The year, month and day on which the federally approved Indirect Cost Rate Agreement ends.
The year, month and day on which the federally approved Indirect Cost Rate Agreement begins.
Circumstances in which the accommodation or change to standards or practices will be applied.
Description of a specific accommodation or change to standards or practices that will be made.
The year, month and day on which the status of an individualized program for a student is significantly altered.
The number of minutes per week that a student with disabilities is served in a regular classroom with his or her non-disabled peers.
The number of minutes per week that a student with disabilities is served in a special education setting separate from his or her non-disabled peers.
The length, in hours, that delivery of the service is planned.
The date the service is planned to begin.
Description of the changes made to the individualized program service plan.
Description of the reason changes were made to the individualized program service plan.
The year, month and day on which the status of the service plan for a child is established or significantly altered.
The year, month and day on which the status of the service plan for a child effectively ends.
The date on which a child's service plan meeting is held.
The position titles of individuals who attend the service plan meeting.
The date parents are notified of the student's progress on annual goals and if the progress is sufficient to achieve the goals by the end of the individualized program service plan.

A description of the progress report used to notify parents of the student's progress on annual goals and if the progress is sufficient to achieve the goals by the end of the individualized program service plan.
Date student will be reevaluated for continued placement in a support program(s).
The year, month and day on which the service plan document is signed.
The position titles of individuals who sign a written service plan.
The first registration term of a person enrolling in credit-granting courses at a postsecondary institution after completing high school (or its equivalent).
The total Title V, Part A funds expended by LEAs.
The total amount of Title V, Part A funds expended by LEAs for the four strategic priorities.
The total Title V, Part A funds received by LEAs.
The year in which the system, component, equipment, or fixture was originally installed.
Unique identification number assigned to postsecondary institutions surveyed through the Integrated Postsecondary Education Data System (IPEDS). Also referred to as UNITID or IPEDS ID.
The number of credit hours and/or contact hours attempted by a person during a term.
The number of instructional minutes in the day in which the school is normally in session.
This provides the next steps for instruction for the student based upon the assessment results and student characteristics.
The dollar amount a school district must pay before its insurance will compensate it for loss.
The beginning date of the time period in which the form is intended to be administered.
The speed that data or information can be downloaded from a server on the internet to one's device in megabits per second.
The date and, optionally, time that the information was gathered.
The speed that data or information can be sent from one's device to another device or server on the internet in megabits per second.
The number of female students who participated on an interscholastic team.  A student should be counted once for each team she was on.
The number of male students who participated on an interscholastic team.  A student should be counted once for each team he was on.
The number of interscholastic sports offered. Sports include distinct sports such as football, basketball, soccer but not intramural sports or cheerleading.
The number of interscholastic sports in which only female students participate.  Sports include distinct sports such as football, basketball, soccer but not intramural sports or cheerleading.
The number of interscholastic sports in which only male students participate.  Sports include distinct sports such as football, basketball, soccer but not intramural sports or cheerleading.

The number of interscholastic teams offered. Teams include each competitive level team in each sport, such as freshman team, junior varsity team, and varsity team but not intramural sports or cheerleading.

The number of interscholastic teams in which only female students participate.  Teams include each competitive level team in each sport, such as freshman team, junior varsity team, and varsity team but not intramural sports or cheerleading.

The number of interscholastic teams in which only male students participate.  Teams include each competitive level team in each sport, such as freshman team, junior varsity team, and varsity team but not intramural sports or cheerleading.
A unique number or alphanumeric code used to identify a job.
The date, determined by the organization, on which they anticipate the selected individual to commence their role within the organization.
A unique number or alphanumeric code used to identify a job position.
The effective date of the job position status.

The year, month and day of the last day of student instruction (including days or times that students are present for purposes of testing and/or evaluation, but not including whole or part-days whose sole purposes is for distribution of report cards).
The year, month and day of the last qualifying move of a migrant student.
The full legal last name borne in common by members of a family.
The north or south angular distance from the equator that, when combined with longitude, reflects an estimation of where the address is physically situated.
A unique identifier for the person performing the learner action.  The identifier should be encrypted when integrating learning experience data across systems to secure the privacy of the learner.
The date and time at which the action was taken.
A description of the object upon which the person has performed the Learner Action.
A globally unique identifier for the object upon which the learning has performed the Learner Action which may be a URL with information about a learning resource or to launch the resource.
The type of object upon which a person has performed the Learner Action.
The value representing input by the learner using an online system, such as a value entered in response to an assessment question, or the URL of a learning resource link clicked.
The creation date of the assignment.
The description and context for the assignment  described in a way that the learner can understand.
The date assignment is due.
The time the assignment is due.
The number attempts a student may make on this assignment. Assumed to be unlimited if zero or omitted.
The time required to complete the assignment.
The number of possible points for an assignment.
The description of the skills or competencies the student must have to engage in assignment.
The date the student was informed about an assignment or that an automated system displays the assignment.
The Uniform Resource Locator pointing to a rubric that may be used to evaluate learner performance on the assignment.
The title for work assigned to the learner, which can comprise of learning resources, activities, and assessments.
The percentage weight of the assignment during the particular course or term.
The Uniform Resource Locator of a learning resource that is an adaptation for this resource.
URL identifier of a learning resource for which this resource is an adaptation.
An email address for the author of the learning resource.
A Uniform Resource Locator (URL) attributed to the author of a learning resource.
A resource that was used in the creation of this resource. This term can be repeated for multiple sources.
The significant topicality of the Learning Resource using free-text keywords and phrases.
The name(s) of the person(s) or organization(s) holding the copyright for the Learning Resource.
The copyright year for the Learning Resource.
The name of a person or organization credited with the creation of the resource.
The date on which the resource was created.
The most recent date that the learning resource was updated.
A short description of the Learning Resource.
A unique number or alphanumeric code assigned to a learning resource by an organization.
A unique number or alphanumeric code assigned to a learning resource by an organization.
The date the learning resource was issued to the student.
The URL where the owner specifies permissions for using the resource.
The date the learning resource was ordered for the student.
The sample size of a peer rating value. Only used when the Peer Rating Value is collected in aggregate as an average of multiple atomic/individual ratings.

An individual score, rating or level assigned to a Learning Resource by a person within the boundaries set by a Peer Rating System that may be aggregated to derive an overall score for the learning resource.
An email address for the publisher of the learning resource.
The name of the organization credited with publishing the resource.
A Uniform Resource Locator (URL) attributed to the publisher of a learning resource.
The date the learning resource was received by the organization that placed the order.
The code used to identify the organization of subject matter and related learning experiences addressed by the learning resource.
The system that is used to identify the organization of subject matter and related learning experiences addressed by the learning resource.
The descriptive name for the subject of the content for the learning resource.
The scaling system used to specify the text complexity of an Learning Resource
The complexity of the text using the scaling system defined by Text Complexity System, e.g. Lexile(tm).
The approximate or typical time it takes to work with or through this learning resource for the typical intended target audience.
The title of the resource.
The maximum for the typical range of ages of the content's intended end user.
The minimum for the typical range of ages of the content's intended end user.
The Uniform Resource Locator where the resource may be accessed, or a proxy for the resource, such as an information page for a commercially available resource.
Defines the version of the learning resource as defined by the publisher.
The total cost of acquiring, owning, operating, and disposing of a building, facility, or piece of equipment over its useful life.
A unique number or alphanumeric code assigned to a local education agency by a school system, a state, or other agency or entity.
The east or west angular distance from the prime meridian that, when combined with latitude, reflects an estimation of where the address is physically situated.
The number of lunch minutes in the day in which the school is normally in session.
The name or description of the marking period (e.g., fall, first marking period).
The date associated with the commencement/entry/start of a role for a person at an organization.
The date associated with the termination/exit/end of a role for a person at an organization.
The date that a Memorandum of Understanding between the Early Learning Organization and the Service Partner is determined to expire.
The effective date that a Memorandum of Understanding between the Early Learning Organization and the Service Partner Organization is effective.
Indicator of student performance at the mid-point of the marking period.
A full legal middle name given to a person at birth, baptism, or through legal change.

The year, month, and day on which the child is no longer eligible for the Migrant Education Program. This date should initially be a date equal to 36 months from the Qualifying Arrival Date to indicate the end of MEP eligibility or the student reaches 22 years of age, whichever comes first.

The qualifying arrival date (QAD) is the month, date, and year that the child completed a move with his or her parent to enable the parent to find qualifying employment. In some cases, the child and worker may not always move together, in which case the QAD would be the date that the child joins the worker who has  already moved, or the date the worker joins the child who has already moved. The QAD is the date the child's eligibility for the Migrant Education Program begins.
The rank a person currently holds as part of active service. Do not use this indicator if the person is no longer holding this rank.
The codes of any activities performed by the person while deployed in the military.
The names of any activities performed by the person while deployed in the military.
A textual description of the person's military deployment.
The date when a person's military deployment ended.
A textual description of the person's military deployment order.
A textual description of an entity that requested the person's military deployment.
The date when a person's military deployment started.
The date when a person was released from further military obligations.
A rank at time a person was discharged from military service.
The highest rank achieved by a person in military service.
A military honor that the person received during the service period.
The date when a person was inducted into active military service.
A rank at the time a person started their military experience.
A military occupational specialty of a person.
The date a person was released from active military service.
A description of the duty stations (locations) encompassed within a period of military service.
The number of minutes in the day in which the school is normally in session.
The date that monitoring visit ended.
The date that monitoring visit began.
The year, month, and day on which the National Center for Education Statistics (NCES) spatially interpolated demographic estimate (SIDE) was processed through the BlindSIDE resource.
The National Center for Education Statistics (NCES) spatially interpolated demographic estimate (SIDE) based on the person's latitude and longitude location.
The standard error applied to the National Center for Education Statistics (NCES) spatially interpolated demographic estimate (SIDE).
The begin year for the American Community Survey (ACS) period estimates used to construct the National Center for Education Statistics (NCES) spatially interpolated demographic estimate (SIDE).
The end year for the American Community Survey (ACS) period estimates used to construct the National Center for Education Statistics (NCES) spatially interpolated demographic estimate (SIDE).
The name of the license/credential awarded by a given profession.

The amount of time necessary for a person to complete all requirements for a degree or certificate according to the institution's catalog. This is typically 4 years (8 semesters or trimesters, or 12 quarters, excluding summer terms) for a bachelor's degree in a standard term-based institution; 2 years (4 semesters or trimesters, or 6 quarters, excluding summer terms) for an associate's degree in a standard term-based institution; and the various scheduled times for certificate programs.
The number of immigrant program [3114(d)(1)] subgrants.
The total number of classrooms for a program, facility, location, or other educational environment.
The number of days a person is absent when school is in session during a given reporting period.
The number of days a person is tardy when school is in session during a given reporting period.
Average number of days for States receiving Title III funds to make subgrants to subgrantees beginning from July 1 of each year, except under conditions where funds are being withheld.
The number of dependents who live with the student and receive more than half their support from them.
Number of child fatalities at the program in the past year, as defined by the State
Number of child injuries at the program in the past year, as defined by the State.
Number of people, including the student, who will attend at least half-time in the current academic year, a program that leads to a college degree or certificate
Total number of persons residing in the same household.
Number of quality levels in the Quality Rating and Improvement System (QRIS).
The year, month and day on which a program or center began operation.
The date on which the individual gave an oral defense.
A unique number or alphanumeric code assigned to an organization by a school, school system, a state, or other agency or entity.
The Uniform Resource Locator (URL) for the unique address of an image representing the Organization.
The total area, measured in square miles, over which an organization holds administrative or governing authority, including regions under its regulatory, operational, or legal control.
The name of a non-person entity such as an organization, institution, agency or business.
The date in which accreditation process was started (but not officially approved or denied)
The type of monitoring on the organization.
The course identifier as it was listed when the credit was earned (e.g. before a system conversion) to show consistency between present transcripts and older ones.
A first name given to a person.
A last name given to a person.
A middle name given to a person.
Previous, alternate or other names or aliases associated with the person.
The amount of money (estimated by the financial aid office) needed by a person to cover expenses such as laundry, transportation, and entertainment.
An indication of the way an academic course was identified at an educational institution.
The date on which the Peer Rating was entered.
The maximum value allowed by the Peer Rating System.
The minimum value allowed by the Peer Rating System.
The name of the scaling system used to specify the Peer Rating.
The optimum value allowed by the Peer Rating System.  The optimum or best rating may be the maximum value, the minimum value, or something in between.
The number of jobs held by a person during the reference period.
A unique number or alphanumeric code assigned to a person by a school, school system, a state, or other agency or entity.
The numeric order in the preferred sequence and priority for contacting a person related to the learner.
Restrictions for student and/or teacher contact with the individual (e.g., the student may not be picked up by the individual)
An appellation, if any, used to denote rank, placement, or status (e.g., Mr., Ms., Reverend, Sister, Dr., Colonel).
The descriptive name of a person's position.
The name or title of the course taken by a student at an academic institution.

The term and year of the initial enrollment of an individual in credit bearing courses or developmental/remedial courses at an institution after completing high school or a high school equivalency program (e.g., GED, Adult High School Diploma).

The total amount institutions estimate that undergraduate-level full-time, first-time degree-seeking students will pay to attend before financial aid is considered. This price includes tuition and fees, books and supplies, room and board, and certain other designated expenses such as transportation. These estimates are the average amounts used by the financial aid office to determine a student's financial aid.
The name of a professional association or organization.
The number issued by the credentialing/licensing agency.
A code given to an activity by an approval organization to designate it as an approved activity.
The latest date that any session of an approved activity may be offered.
The earliest date that any session of an approved activity may be offered.
A code assigned to an professional development activity by the organization offering the activity that is unique to the non-variable activity details.
The cost for an attendee to participate in a professional development activity.
The number of credits a professional development activity provides.
A description of the content covered in the professional development activity.
The year, month, and day on which any certificate awarded as part of a professional development activity expires.
The expected outcomes of a participant in an activity.
The primary source of funding for a professional development session.
The date the professional development plan is completed.
The total number of participants that can be accommodated by a professional development session.
The year, month and day a professional development session ends.
The time at which a professional development session ends.
The method used to evaluate a professional development session.
The score or rating used to determine if a professional development session was successful.
The unique, non-duplicated,  identification number assigned by the registry data system for a session of a particular professional development activity.
The name of a location where a professional development session will be held.
The year, month, and day a professional development session begins.
The time at which a professional development session begins.
A unique number or alphanumeric code assigned to a program by a school, school system, a state, or other agency or entity.
The normal length in credit/contact hours of a person's program as published in the institution's catalogue, website, or other official documents.
The name of the program of instruction, training, services or benefits available through federal, state, or local agencies.
The state defined status assigned to the school's performance on the progress of achieving the English language proficiency indicator.
The year and month the student is projected to graduate.
The number of instructional hours completed by an adult enrolled in a distance learning program.

The dollar amount spent on transportation for public school choice during the school year under Title I of ESEA as amended, Part A, Section 1116.
The name of the city in which the child resided prior to the qualifying move.
The maximum score option for the QRIS or other quality initiative.
The minimum score option for the QRIS or other quality initiative.

The quality initiative start date.

The number of recess minutes in the day in which the school is normally in session.
A unique number or alphanumeric code assigned to a record.
The date of referral.
The reason for the referral.
The person, program, or organization making the initial referral.
A type of service that a child or family has received a referral for.
The program or organization to which the child/family was referred.
An indication of the competency definition(s) addressed in the Course Section.
Identifies the reporter of the incident using  a pre-existing unique student identifier or unique staff identifier, when the reporter is a student or staff member.
Fixed sum charged to persons for items not covered by tuition and required of such a large proportion of all students that the student who does not pay the charge is the exception.
Number of clock hours of training required for providers to meet requirements of the state.
The district responsible for specific educational services and/or instruction of the student.
Identifies an organization responsible for specific educational services and/or instruction based on a type of responsibility  specified in the Responsible Organization Type.
The school responsible for specific education services and/or instruction of the student.
The charges for an academic year for rooming accommodations for a typical student sharing a room with one other student.
A textual label for category by which Rubric Criterion may be grouped.
Text describing a criterion that must be met to demonstrate quality for a product, process, or performance task.
Text describing  one or more benchmarks that must be met to achieve a degree of achievement on a product, process, or performance task.
Pre-defined feedback text to be relayed to the person or organization being evaluated.  This may include guidance and suggestions for improvement or development.
A numeric value representing the level's position in the list of levels defined for the Rubric Criterion.
A qualitative description of this degree of achievement used for column headers or row labels in tabular rubrics.
